Public Works Commission Meeting 06-22-22 Item 4.A.

Public Works Commission Agenda Report

To: Chair Major and Members of the Public Works Commission Prepared by: Troy Spayd, Assistant Public Works Director/City Engineer Approved by: Rob DuBoux, Public Works Director/City Engineer Date prepared:   June 15, 2022 Meeting date: June 22, 2022 Subject: Capital Improvement Projects and Disaster Recovery Projects Status Report
RECOMMENDED ACTION: Receive and file report on the status of the City’s current and upcoming Capital Improvements Projects and Disaster Recovery Projects.

DISCUSSION:  Staff will provide a status update on the following active projects in the Fiscal Year 2021-2022 Capital Improvement Program:

Capital Improvement Projects (Design)

Marie Canyon Green Streets

¾ Updates:

  A  bid  opening  was  held  on  June  9,  2022,  and  the  agreement  is scheduled for the July 11, 2022 Council meeting.

¾ Project Schedule:

 Complete Design: May 2022  Begin Construction: Summer 2022

PCH Median Improvements Project

¾

Updates:  The project was approved by the Planning Commission on September 8,

 2021. This project received Caltrans approval since the work will be on  Pacific  Coast  Highway.  The  project  will  be  advertised  for construction  bids  after  approval.

An  agreement  for  construction management services was approved by Council on March 14, 2022. The  project  will  go  out  to  bid  once  the  specifications  have  been finalized.Page 1 of 8


Agenda Item # 4.A.

¾

Project Schedule:  Complete Design: March 2022  Advertise:

 Summer 2022

 Begin Construction: Fall 2022

PCH Signal Synchronization System Improvements Project

y ¾ Updates:

 This project will be presented to the Planning Commission in July 2022.

 This project requires Caltrans approval since the work will be on PCH.

The project report has been approved and final plans are being routed through Caltrans for approval.  It is anticipated that the project will have final  approval  by  June  2022.    The  project  will  be  advertised  for construction  bids  shortly  after  final  approval.

    If  possible,  the construction  of  this  project  will  begin  in  coordination  with  the  PCH

Median Improvement.

¾ Project Schedule:  Complete Final Design: Spring 2022

 Advertise: Summer 2022  Award Contract and Begin Construction:

 Fall 2022

Westward Beach Road Repair Project

¾ Updates:

Updates:  On March 28, 2022,

 The City Council modified the scope of work for this project to include the repair of the existing road as necessary. The proposed revision makes the Measure M funding not eligible for this project.
The  revised  project  budget  was  included  in  the  Fiscal  Year 2022-2023  budget.    Upon  the  adoption  of  the  budget,  the  revised project will be redesigned and constructed early fall.

¾ Project Schedule:  Complete Design: Summer 2022

 Begin Construction: Fall 2022

Civic Center Water Treatment Facility Phase 2

¾ Updates:

 At the June 13, 2022 City Council meeting,

 the Council approved an Amendment to the HRL agreement to provide additional design budget. The additional budget will be used to fund the cultural resources soil investigation.  This work is required with the SRF financing.

 Letters  will  be  mailed  out  the  property  owners  providing  a  project update regarding the proposed time extension to the MOU deadlines.

 Staff is working with the State Water Board for available project grants. ¾

Project Schedule:

 Complete Design: December 2021  Advertise for Bidding:

 December 2022

Permanent Skate Park

Permanent Skate Park ¾ Project  Description:

    This  project  includes  designing  and  constructing  a permanent skate park located on the Crummer/Case Court parcel adjacent to Malibu Bluffs Park.  The project would include parking and additional site amenities such as trash cans, benches, tables, and restrooms.

¾ Updates:

 In May 2021,

 the Council approved funding for additional engineering work related to the project. Staff has worked with the consultant over the past several months to complete the engineering work, and the final draft plans are expected to be completed in Spring 2022. The Planning Commission will then review the project in Summer 2022 before final review by the Council.

¾ Estimated Schedule:  Complete Design: Spring 2022

 Begin Construction:  To be determined

¾ Project Description:

  This project consists of installing a new westbound right turn lane at the intersection of PCH and Trancas Canyon Road.

¾ Updates:  The agreement was awarded at the Council on May 9,

 2022.

A kick-off meeting was held on May 16, 2022 and the design is underway. ¾

Capital Improvement Projects (Not Started)

Vehicle Protection Devices ¾ Project Description:  Per Malibu Municipal Code Section 17.

48.070 requires vehicle impact protection devices to be installed for all parking spaces located adjacent  to  any  outdoor  pedestrian  seating  area.    There  are  two  locations within the City’s right-of-way  and City-owned property that would require a vehicle impact protection device.

Legacy Park Paver Repair Project

¾ Project Description:

  This project will consist of removing and repairing a large section  of  pavers  in  Legacy  Park.  The  pavers  have  become  uneven  and damaged in several areas

Malibu Bluffs Park South Walkway

¾ Project Description:

  This project will include replacing the existing sidewalk located on the south side of Malibu Bluffs Park.

Trancas Playground Resurfacing

¾ Project Description:

  The project will include removing and replacing the upper layer of the playground surfacing at Trancas Canyon Park.

PCH Median Improvements at Paradise Cove and Zuma Beach ¾ Project Description:

  This project was identified in the 2015 PCH Safety Study and includes installing new raised medians and improvements.  New raised medians are proposed east and west of PCH and Paradise Cove Road.

The proposed improvements also include the relocation of the existing bus stop and new signage.  The project will also include the installation of new raised
Page 4 of 8
Agenda Item # 4.A.

medians on PCH in the areas where the double yellow lines exist in the vicinity of Zuma Beach, specifically where the yellow paddles are installed.

Capital Improvement Projects (Completed)

Civic Center Way Improvements

Updates:  The  contractor  completed  the  project  and  Council  accepted  this  project  as complete at the September 13th City Council Meeting.

Civic Center Stormwater Diversion Structure

Updates: The contractor has completed the storm drain improvements on Civic  Center Way.

The improvements modified the existing concrete channel underneath Civic Center Way  by  adding  a  3’  concrete  curb  adjacent  to  the  existing  flap  gates.    The  new  curb prevents  brackish  water  intrusion  into  the  City’s  Civic  Center  Stormwater  Treatment Facility.

 This project was accepted by the Council at the January 24, 2022 meeting.

2021 Annual Street Maintenance

Updates:  This  project  included  resurfacing  Malibu  Road,  Broad  Beach  Road,  Latigo Canyon  Road,  Corral  Canyon  Road,  Webb  Way,  Rambla  Pacifico  Street  and  Vista Pacifica with a slurry seal treatment and adding speed humps to Birdview Avenue.  This project was identified in the City’s Pavement Management Plan.

This project was accepted by the Council at the January 24, 2022 meeting.

Storm Drain Master Plan (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project is funded through a grant from FEMA after the Woolsey Fire.  The City will create a complete inventory of storm drains, culverts, debris basins, manholes, and other drainage structures within the City.

¾ Updates:  Council approved an agreement in December for design services.

A kick-off meeting was held in late December.

¾ Estimated Schedule:  Completion Date:  Summer 2022

Clover Heights Storm Drain (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project consists of design and construction of a few storm drains on Clover Heights.  The existing storm drain facility ends at the intersection  of  Clover  Heights  and  Harvester  Road.    During  storms,  this intersection floods and causes debris to block the road.

An extended storm drain towards the end of Clover Heights will help eliminate this issue.

¾ Updates:

 An agreement was awarded at the June 13, 2022 Council meeting.

  A kick-off meeting has been scheduled with the Consultant to begin the design process.

Encinal Canyon Road Drainage Improvements (CalOES Project) ¾ Project Description:

  This project consists of repairing damage storm drain facilities and roadway embankments that were damaged by the Woolsey Fire.

¾ Updates:

 The  project  design  has  been  completed  at  90%  and  consultant  is completing the final design.

¾ Estimated Schedule:  Complete Design: Summer 2022  Begin Construction:

 Fall 2022

Malibu Road Slope Repairs (CalOES Project)

¾ Project Description:

  The existing slope adjacent to the beach access stairs at 24712 Malibu Road has been eroded and caused damage to Malibu Road.

¾ Updates:

 The  design  has  started  and  is  anticipated  to  be  completed  by  the Summer 2022.

Westward Beach Road Shoulder Repairs (CalOES Project)

¾ Project Description:  The project consists of repairs the damaged shoulder on Westward Beach Road.

¾ Updates:

 This  project  is  currently  under  design  with  the  City’s  consultant.
It  is anticipated  that  the  final  design  will  be  complete  by  Fall  2022.  The project  will  be  advertised  for  construction  bids  with  construction beginning in Spring 2023.

¾ Estimated Schedule:  Complete Design: Fall 2022  Begin Construction:

 Spring 2023

Outdoor Warning Sirens - Design (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  An Emergency Warning Siren system will improve the City’s  ability  to  alert  the  community  by  providing  mechanisms  to  alert residents in areas without power and those who live in neighborhoods with poor  cell  service.

Phase  One  of  the  project  included  a  sound  study  to determine the optimal number and locations of individual sirens, power and infrastructure  requirements,  and  siren  sound  range.

    The  City  has  been awarded a FEMA Hazard Mitigation grant to fund the design, engineering and environmental compliance needed for a shovel ready project.

Phase Two of the Emergency Warning Sirens project will be implementation of the design plan,  including  purchasing,  installing,  and  testing  the  sirens.    The  City  will apply for future grant funding for this phase of the project. ¾ Updates:

 On  November  30,  2021,

  City  Council  approved  an  agreement  with Acoustic Technology to design the outdoor warning sirens.

A design report will be presented to Council upon completion.

 Comments  were  sent  back  to  the  Consultant,

  a  meeting  will  be scheduled to discuss those comments with the consultant in the next few days.

¾ Estimated Schedule: TBD
